case_title: RANDY SCHRECK VS. NEA DELIVERY LLC DBA AIRSPACE TECHNOLOGIES, INC. ET AL

docket_entries.description: DECLARATION OF CHRISTOPHER NEPACENA IN SUPPORT OF EX PARTE APPLICATION FOR ORDER TO CONTINUE TRIAL AND ALL RELATED DATES (TRANSACTION ID # 63081171) FILED BY DEFENDANT FLORES JR, DAVID ANTHONY NEA DELIVERY LLC DBA FIRST DELIVERY SERVICE (ERRONEOUSLY SUED AS NEA DELIVERY LLC DBA AIRSPACE TECHONOLOGIES, INC.)

STIPULATION TO CONTINUE TRIAL DATE (TRANSACTION ID # 63081171) FILED BY FLORES JR, DAVID ANTHONY NEA DELIVERY LLC DBA FIRST DELIVERY SERVICE (ERRONEOUSLY SUED AS NEA DELIVERY LLC DBA AIRSPACE TECHONOLOGIES, INC.)
MSC SETTING REVIEW CONTINUED

DISCOVERY, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE JUL-18-2019 HEARING RE: DEFENDANT NEA DELIVERY LLC DBA FIRST DELIVERY SERVICE'S MOTION TO COMPEL PSYCH IME OF PLAINTIFF RANDY SCHRECK, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: THE MOTION FOR A MENTAL EXAMINATION IS DENIED. DEFENDANTS DO NOT SHOW GOOD CAUSE UNDER CCP 2032.320(A). WHILE PLAINTIFF ALLEGES ANXIETY AND OTHER NEUROLOGICAL DAMAGES FOLLOWING THE ACCIDENT, DEFENDANTS DO NOT ARTICULATE WHY THEY NEED A MENTAL EXAMINATION IN ADDITION TO ROUTINE DISCOVERY OF MEDICAL RECORDS. MOREOVER, THE MOVING PAPERS AND MEET-AND-CONFER CORRESPONDENCE WERE UNCLEAR WHAT DIAGNOSTIC TESTS OR PROCEDURES WERE TO BE DONE, OR WHY THOSE PROCEDURES WOULD LEAD TO EVIDENCE THAT WOULD BE RELEVANT TO ANY CLAIMS OR DEFENSES. CCP 2032.320(D). DEFENDANTS SUBMISSION OF DR. STASSBERG'S DECLARATION WITH THE REPLY IS IMPROPER, ESPECIALLY CONSIDERING THAT THE INFORMATION IN THAT DECLARATION COULD AND SHOULD HAVE BEEN PROVIDED IN THE MEET AND CONFER PROCESS. JAY V. MAHAFFEY (2013) 218 CAL.APP.4TH 1522, 1537-38. THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE PRO TEM: SCOTT BORROWMAN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; NOT REPORTED (302/JPT)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Nov-04-2019 Vacated

Notice Of Motion To Deem Requests Admitted And Compel Responses To Discovery
Off Calendar Sep-23-2019 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

There being no appearance, the tentative ruling is adopted as follows:

PLAINTIFF RANDY SCHRECK's Motion To Deem Requests Admitted And Compel Responses To Discovery is off calendar. Case settled.

Judge Pro Tem: Sigrid Irias; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Psych Ime Of Plaintiff Randy Schreck
Off Calendar Jul-18-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances are made by or for any party in the case.

DEFENDANT NEA DELIVERY LLC DBA FIRST DELIVERY SERVICE's Motion To Compel Psych Ime Of Plaintiff Randy Schreck.

The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: The motion for a mental examination is DENIED. Defendants do not show good cause under CCP 2032.320(a). While Plaintiff alleges anxiety and other neurological damages following the accident, Defendants do not articulate why they need a mental examination in addition to routine discovery of medical records. Moreover, the moving papers and meet-and-confer correspondence were unclear what diagnostic tests or procedures were to be done, or why those procedures would lead to evidence that would be relevant to any claims or defenses. CCP 2032.320(d). Defendants submission of Dr. Stassberg's declaration with the reply is improper, especially considering that the information in that declaration could and should have been provided in the meet and confer process. Jay v. Mahaffey (2013) 218 Cal.App.4th 1522, 1537-38.

The prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order which complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge Pro Tem: Scott Borrowman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/JPT)
